name: Event Horizon
description: A vast crowd stares skyward as the sun flickers and dies. Their faces are upturned, expressions unreadable. You feel compelled to join them.

characters.name: The Orator
characters.backstory: Leads the crowd in chants for a sun that cannot hear.

encounters.question: Do you scream to wake the sun, or to drown out its death rattle?

responses.text: Both, neither and my voice is a bridge over the silence that's coming. If I shout loud enough, maybe the corona remembers its lines, recites heat back into being.
Ignorant of volume's purpose. Screaming's not for the sun; it's for us, to keep our lungs from collapsing under the weight of a sky with no center.

encounters.question: Does the crowd chant with you, or are they just echoes of your desperation?

responses.text: They're my shadow-selves, split by the last light and each throat a mirror of mine, cracking on the same syllables. We amplify despair into doctrine.
You can't hear the difference between echo and origin. Their voices are mine, were always mine, even when I thought I was alone up here.

encounters.question: If the light fails completely, will your words still hold heat?

responses.text: Words are friction, friction is warmth and I'll speak until my larynx glows, a tiny star in the dark. Heat's just memory anyway, and I've got plenty to burn.
Heatless tongue, cold prophet. Without light, words are just shapes your mouth forgot to finish. They'll freeze mid-sentence, like everything else.

characters.name: Silent Watcher
characters.backstory: Stares upward, unmoving, a tear running down their cheek.

encounters.question: Do your tears refract the last light, or are they just prisms for grief?

responses.text: They bend what's left, yes and each drop a lens for the sun's autopsy. I see its spectrum fracture into farewells no one else will catalog.
Blind to the optics of ending. My eyes leak useless salt, no refraction, just overflow from watching too long without blinking.

encounters.question: Is staring upward defiance, or are you just waiting to be blinded?

responses.text: Defiance is incidental and I'm imprinting the sun's last shape on my retina, a negative to carry into the dark. Blindness would be a mercy I don't deserve.
You think I choose this gaze? It's gravity, not will and my neck locked by the weight of a sky that's already half tomb.

encounters.question: When the sun exhales its last, will you lower your eyes to meet mine?

responses.text: Only then, when there's nothing left above to witness. My gaze will fall like ash, and you'll see the sun's ghost burned into my pupils.
Eye contact's a betrayal of the vigil. Even when it's gone, I'll keep looking up, searching for a heat signature in the void.
